item.title: My New Page
item.description:
My research interests include the political economy of communication & media, critical communication research, ICTs, digital labor & gig worker, platform economy, algorithmic management, human-computer/AI interaction, algorithmic fairness, accountability, transparency, and ethics (FATE), and mixed methods research.

Publications

Peer-reviewed Articles

To be updated.

Book Chapters

[1] Fu, T. (in preparation). Between ghostwriting, guidance, and co-creation: Navigating transparency in human–LLM teams for consulting. In C. W. Piercy, & A. C. Zanin (Eds.) Teaming with machines: How contemporary teams collaborate with complex work technologies. Wiley-Blackwell Publishing.

Email: tianyangfu@utexas.edu
School Email: tf9368@eid.utexas.edu
Personal Website: tianyangfu.github.io

Many thanks for your interest 🖤

Education

The University of Texas at Austin (UT Austin)
Ph.D. in Communication Studies, Organizational Communication & Technology
Graduate Portfolio Program in Ethical AI
Advisor: Dr. Dawna Ballard
The University of Chicago
M.A. in Social Sciences, concentration on Sociology
Advisor: Dr. Andreas Glaeser
Beijing Foreign Studies University (BFSU)
B.A. in Journalism, concentration on Intercultural Communication
Minor: International Economics & Trade
Advisor: Dr. Fei Jiang (University of Chinese Academy of Social Sciences)
The University of Edinburgh
Study Abroad

Aside from research, I enjoy teaching and tutoring tudents with subjects across disciplines as a Teaching Assistant or Assistant Instructor. Selected teaching experiences include:

At the University of Texas at Austin:

  • CMS 341 Digital Communications (Prof. Natalie B. Andreas; SU26)
  • CMS 345P Communication & Public Opinion (Prof. Talia Stroud; SP26)
  • CMS 388 Leadership Stories (Prof. Joe Cutbirth; SP26)
  • CMS 399K Working Virtually (Prof. Natalie B. Andreas; FA25)
  • CMS 372T Time Matters (Prof. Dawna Ballard; FA24, SP25)

All other institutions:
